entries.name: Inbox
entries.description: Facilitates sending L1 to L2 messages like depositing ETH, but does not escrow funds.

entries.name: RollupProxy
entries.description: Central contract for the project's configuration like its execution logic hash (`wasmModuleRoot`) and addresses of the other system contracts. Entry point for Proposers creating new assertions (state commitments) and Challengers submitting fraud proofs (In the Orbit stack, these two roles are both called Validators).

minimumAssertionPeriod.description: Minimum time delta between newly created nodes (stateUpdates). This is checked on `stakeOnNewNode()`. Format is number of ETHEREUM blocks, even for L3s.

confirmPeriodBlocks.description: Challenge period. (Number of ETHEREUM blocks until a node is confirmed, even for L3s).

wasmModuleRoot.description: Root hash of the WASM module used for execution, like a fingerprint of the L2 logic. Can be associated with ArbOS versions.

arbOsFromWmRoot.description: ArbOS version derived from known wasmModuleRoots.

entries.name: OneStepProverMath
entries.description: One of the modular contracts used for the last step of a fraud proof, which is simulated inside a WASM virtual machine.

entries.name: OneStepProofEntry
entries.description: One of the modular contracts used for the last step of a fraud proof, which is simulated inside a WASM virtual machine.

entries.name: UpgradeExecutor
entries.description: Central contract defining the access control permissions for upgrading the system contract implementations.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: Pause and unpause and set important roles and parameters in the system contracts: Can delegate Sequencer management to a BatchPosterManager address, manage data availability and DACs, set the Sequencer-only window, introduce an allowList to the bridge and whitelist Inboxes/Outboxes.

entries.name: OneStepProverMemory
entries.description: One of the modular contracts used for the last step of a fraud proof, which is simulated inside a WASM virtual machine.

entries.name: L1GatewayRouter
entries.description: This routing contract maps tokens to the correct escrow (gateway) to be then bridged with canonical messaging.

entries.name: EdgeChallengeManager
entries.description: Contract that implements the main challenge protocol logic of the fraud proof system.

entries.name: OneStepProver0
entries.description: One of the modular contracts used for the last step of a fraud proof, which is simulated inside a WASM virtual machine.

receivedPermissions.description: Pause and unpause and set important roles and parameters in the system contracts: Can delegate Sequencer management to a BatchPosterManager address, manage data availability and DACs, set the Sequencer-only window, introduce an allowList to the bridge and whitelist Inboxes/Outboxes.
manage all access control roles.

entries.name: L1ERC20Gateway
entries.description: Escrows deposited ERC-20 assets for the canonical Bridge. Upon depositing, a generic token representation will be minted at the destination. Withdrawals are initiated by the Outbox contract.

receivedPermissions.description: Can propose new state roots (called nodes) and challenge state roots on the host chain.
Can propose new state roots (called nodes) and challenge state roots on the host chain.

entries.name: SequencerInbox
entries.description: A sequencer (registered in this contract) can submit transaction batches or commitments here.

maxTimeVariation.description: Settable by the Rollup Owner. Transactions can only be force-included after the `delayBlocks` window (Sequencer-only) has passed.

receivedPermissions.description: cancel queued transactions.
propose transactions.

entries.name: RollupEventInbox
entries.description: Helper contract sending configuration data over the bridge during the systems initialization.

receivedPermissions.description: Can submit transaction batches or commitments to the SequencerInbox contract on the host chain.

entries.name: Bridge
entries.description: Escrow contract for the project's gas token (can be different from ETH). Keeps a list of allowed Inboxes and Outboxes for canonical bridge messaging.

allowedOutboxList.description: Can make calls as the bridge, steal all funds.

outboxHistory.description: All Outboxes that were ever set as allowed in the bridge.

allowedDelayedInboxList.description: Allowed to mint the gastoken on L2 and call `enqueueDelayedMessage()` on the bridge.

inboxHistory.description: All Inboxes that were ever set as allowed in the bridge.

entries.name: OneStepProverHostIo
entries.description: One of the modular contracts used for the last step of a fraud proof, which is simulated inside a WASM virtual machine.

entries.name: TimelockController
entries.description: A timelock with access control. The current minimum delay is 7d.

receivedPermissions.description: Pause and unpause and set important roles and parameters in the system contracts: Can delegate Sequencer management to a BatchPosterManager address, manage data availability and DACs, set the Sequencer-only window, introduce an allowList to the bridge and whitelist Inboxes/Outboxes.
manage all access control roles.
manage all access control roles.

entries.name: Outbox
entries.description: Facilitates L2 to L1 contract calls: Messages initiated from L2 (for example withdrawal messages) eventually resolve in execution on L1.

receivedPermissions.description: Can add or remove transaction filterers and change ArbOS configuration on the L2, without delay.
execute transactions that are ready.
manage all access control roles.
manage all access control roles.

entries.name: ArbFilteredTransactionsManager
entries.description: ArbOS 61 transaction-filtering precompile. An authorized filterer registers tx hashes here; the state transition function then forcibly fails those transactions, including force-included ones, without delay.

entries.name: L2UpgradeExecutor
entries.description: ArbOS chain owner (UpgradeExecutor). Manages the ArbOwner chain-owner set and the transaction-filterer set, and can upgrade ArbOS configuration without delay.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: Can add or remove transaction filterers and change ArbOS configuration on the L2, without delay.

receivedPermissions.description: mint an uncapped amount of any connected Stock Token to any non-blocked address while the token is not paused.
cancel queued transactions.
propose transactions.
deploy and initialize new Stock Token beacon proxies with arbitrary product identifiers, names and symbols through the StockFactory.

entries.name: TimelockController
entries.description: A timelock with access control. The current minimum delay is 7d.

receivedPermissions.description: Can add or remove transaction filterers and change ArbOS configuration on the L2, without delay.
manage all access control roles.
upgrade the external StockFactory that deploys and initializes new Stock Tokens.
Can add or remove transaction filterers and change ArbOS configuration on the L2, without delay.
manage all access control roles.
burn arbitrary holders' balances without allowance on any connected Stock Token while neither the caller nor holder is blocked and the token is not paused.
toggle the oraclePaused flag on any connected Stock Token; the current implementation does not enforce this flag internally.
block and unblock arbitrary addresses across all Stock Tokens, preventing them from sending, receiving, approving, spending or submitting permits.
set or schedule any nonzero UI multiplier for any unpaused Stock Token; standard ERC-20 balances and supply are unaffected.
confiscate arbitrary holders' balances without allowance on any connected Stock Token, even when the token is paused or either address is blocked.
change the name and symbol of any connected Stock Token, which also changes its EIP-712 permit domain.
replace the implementation used by all Stock Token beacon proxies without a delay.

entries.name: NVIDIA • Robinhood Token
entries.description: ERC-20-compatible Robinhood Stock Token logic. Transfers, approvals and permits are permissionless for addresses that are not blocked in the shared AccessControlsRegistry; there is no onchain KYC or allowlist. The registry's roles can mint, burn arbitrary holders' balances, confiscate balances even while paused or blocked, pause this token or all tokens, change metadata and the UI multiplier, and upgrade the shared beacon implementation.

ACCESS_CONTROLLED_REGISTRY.description: Shared access-control registry and upgrade beacon. It stores the global pause and blocklist and supplies every privileged role used by this token.

paused.description: Whether ordinary token operations are paused, either locally or through the shared registry. A pause disables transfers, approvals, permits, minting, regular burning and multiplier updates, but not adminBurn(), metadata changes or oracle-flag changes.

tokenPaused.description: Whether this token is locally paused. The effective paused state also includes the registry-wide pause.

oraclePaused.description: Oracle-status flag controlled by ORACLE_PAUSER_ROLE. This flag is not consulted by any function in this token implementation and has no direct effect on ERC-20 operations.

uiMultiplier.description: Current multiplier used only by balanceOfUI(), totalSupplyUI() and TransferWithScaledUI events. It does not rebase or change standard ERC-20 balances or total supply.

newUIMultiplier.description: Pending UI multiplier, which can be set to any nonzero value by MULTIPLIER_UPDATER_ROLE.

effectiveAt.description: Timestamp at which newUIMultiplier becomes the active UI multiplier. It cannot be scheduled in the past.

terms.description: Hardcoded URL for the legal terms associated with Robinhood Assets (Jersey) Stock Tokens.

uid.description: Immutable-per-proxy product identifier assigned during initialization.

totalSupply.description: Standard ERC-20 supply. It can be increased without an onchain cap by MINTER_ROLE and reduced from arbitrary accounts by BURNER_ROLE or ADMIN_BURNER_ROLE.

receivedPermissions.description: grant and revoke every registry and Stock Token role, including the ability to grant these powers to themselves.

entries.name: AccessControlsRegistry
entries.description: Shared access-control registry and upgrade beacon for Robinhood Stock Tokens. Its roles apply across every token implementation that points to this registry: they control upgrades, global and per-token pauses, the shared blocklist, issuance, arbitrary holder burns, metadata and UI multipliers.

implementation.description: Implementation used by every beacon proxy pointing to this registry.

paused.description: Registry-wide pause. When true, all connected Stock Tokens treat themselves as paused.

blockedAccounts.description: Addresses in the shared denylist, reconstructed from Blocked and Unblocked events. A blocked sender, receiver, spender or transaction caller cannot use transfers, approvals or permits on any connected token.

accessControl.description: Current admin relationships and membership for every registry and Stock Token role.

receivedPermissions.description: pause and unpause ordinary operations across all Stock Tokens connected to this registry.

Can register/remove transaction hashes in the ArbFilteredTransactionsManager precompile, causing the state transition function to forcibly fail those transactions, including force-included ones, without delay.
pause and unpause any individual Stock Token connected to this registry.
